pi_items.metadata_value: Contamination assessments, remediation plans, community health studies at Superfund sites
pi_items.verified_value: Relevant to Cook County Superfund sites
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:16-18. EPA and state environmental databases confirm Cook County, Illinois has 4 active Superfund sites (Celotex, Lake Calumet Cluster, Schroud Property, Heart of Chicago). These sites have documented soil and groundwater contamination.

pi_items.verified_value: Cook County has 4 active Superfund NPL sites
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh:18. EPA Superfund database and news sources confirm Cook County has multiple Superfund sites including Celotex Corporation, Lake Calumet Cluster, Schroud Property, and Heart of Chicago Area Soils Site.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic test data in a development ArkCase environment. The login credentials (arkcase-admin@dev.arkcase.com / ArkCase1234!) are verified test credentials created during Kubernetes/Helm ArkCase deployment using the dev.arkcase.com LDAP domain. The complaint references a FOIA request with number EPA-HQ-SFUND-2023-000847 allegedly received from Earthjustice Legal Defense Fund on 2023-02-28. While Earthjustice is verified as a real nonprofit environmental law organization that actively submits FOIA requests, the specific request number EPA-HQ-SFUND-2023-000847 was not found in EPA FOIA logs, public records, or databases. Cook County, Illinois is verified to have multiple active Superfund sites with documented contamination, making the geographic location and subject matter realistic, but the specific FOIA case appears to be synthetic.
